Fix unhandled promise rejection in InsertNewPageDialog.openDialog() (#776)

After PR #774 modified `page-chat-panel.ts`, the frontend CI exited with
code 1 despite all extension tests passing — because `web-test-runner`
was reporting a failing assertion in `page-chat-panel.test.ts` (tests
still expected the old `error`-setting behavior, not the new
`pendingMessage` queuing behavior). That test fix landed in PR #773.

This PR addresses a second issue visible in CI logs from both the
failing and now-passing runs: an unhandled `TypeError` fired from a
fire-and-forget `.then()` in `openDialog()`.

## Root cause

`automagic-identifier-input` is only type-imported in
`insert-new-page-dialog.ts`, so in tests the custom element is never
upgraded. The shadow DOM query returns an unupgraded `HTMLElement`,
which has no `focusTitleInput` method. The existing `?.` guard only
protected against `null`/`undefined` — not against the property being
`undefined` on a live element:

```typescript
// Before — throws TypeError if element not upgraded
this.updateComplete.then(() => {
  const identifierInput = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<AutomagicIdentifierInput>('automagic-identifier-input');
  identifierInput?.focusTitleInput();  // 💥 not a function
});
```

## Fix

Replace the fire-and-forget `.then()` with `await this.updateComplete`
inside the `async` method, and use optional-call syntax (`?.()`) to
defensively handle the unupgraded case:

```typescript
// After — awaited, no unhandled rejection, safe if not upgraded
await this.updateComplete;
const identifierInput = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<AutomagicIdentifierInput>('automagic-identifier-input');
identifierInput?.focusTitleInput?.();
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: brendanjerwin <3039+brendanjerwin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add rate-limit, ip-allowlist, and security-headers middlewares

Three new Traefik middlewares in the authentik namespace:

1. rate-limit: 30 req/min avg, 50 burst — for auth-heavy endpoints
2. ip-allowlist: restricts to home LAN (192.168.68.0/24) + k3s CIDRs
   Use on admin tools: ArgoCD, Longhorn, Prometheus
3. security-headers: strict CSP, HSTS preload, frame-deny, XSS filter,
   permissions-policy, strips Server/X-Powered-By headers

Usage in IngressRoutes:
  middlewares:
    - name: authentik-auth
      namespace: authentik
    - name: ip-allowlist       # add for admin tools
      namespace: authentik
    - name: security-headers   # add for stricter headers
      namespace: authentik

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove double-fetch in Timeline.OnAfterRenderAsync (#43)

The OnAfterRenderAsync override re-fetched data after PostPageBase already
loaded page 0. With cursor-based pagination (PR #41), the second fetch used
stale cursor values and returned page 1 data instead of page 0, breaking
infinite scroll on first scroll-down.

Remove the override  the base class handles initial loading correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix speeder centering: invert offset to account for PI rotation

After rotating the model root PI around Y, the child's [2,0,1.5]
offset becomes [-2,0,-1.5] in world space. Compensating with negative
values doubled the error. Use positive offset so they cancel to zero.

Fix speeder model centering and facing direction

Kenney GLBs have the mesh child at translation [2, 0, 1.5] in local
space. After scaling by 0.5 this offset becomes (1.0, 0, 0.75), placing
the model off-center on the tile. Compensate with a matching negative
position on the model root.

Also rotate model PI (180 deg) around Y: the speeder faces -Z in the
GLB but our direction code treats +Z as forward (rotation.y=0 -> +Z).

fix: three bugs found during quality sprint (#193)

1. parse_step_type: accept 'bash' and 'command' as aliases for Shell
   The Python side accepts 'bash' as a step type but the Rust parser only
   mapped 'shell'. Steps with type: bash would silently fall through to
   inference, which is fragile.

2. Event factory functions: use meaningful source_id instead of random UUID
   All make_*_event functions hardcoded source_id to Uuid::new_v4(), losing
   the caller's identity. Now: make_learn_content_event uses source param,
   make_agent_ready_event uses agent_id, make_query_response_event takes an
   explicit agent_id parameter, make_query_event defaults to 'eval-coordinator'
   with a new make_query_event_from variant for custom sources.

3. run_eval: add run_eval_with_responder for LocalEventBus compatibility
   run_eval publishes a query then immediately polls — with LocalEventBus,
   no agents exist to respond, so collect_responses always returned empty.
   The new run_eval_with_responder accepts an EvalResponder callback invoked
   between publish and poll, enabling local simulation. run_eval is unchanged
   (wraps the new function with None).

5064 tests passing, 0 failures.

commit.message:
Replace OFFSET pagination with cursor-based pagination (#41)

Use (PublishDateOrder, Id) as a composite cursor for keyset pagination.
OFFSET scales linearly with page depth (page 6 = 613ms). Cursor-based
pagination seeks directly via index  O(1) regardless of depth.

Backend:
- SQLiteItemRepository: cursor WHERE clause when lastPublishDateOrder
  and lastId are provided, falls back to OFFSET for page 0
- Updated ORDER BY to PublishDateOrder DESC, Id DESC for deterministic
  cursor ordering
- DROP/CREATE idx_items_timeline with (UserId, PublishDateOrder DESC,
  Id DESC) for cursor tie-breaking
- ItemController: added cursorPublishDateOrder/cursorId query params,
  enforces both-or-neither, removed .OrderByDescending().ToHashSet()
  that was destroying SQL sort order

Frontend:
- PostPageBase: tracks CursorPublishDateOrder/CursorId from last item,
  centralized ResetPagination() clears cursor on filter change
- FeedClient: passes cursor params in timeline/feed URLs
- Timeline/Posts/Search pages pass cursor to API calls

Tests:
- Cursor pagination returns correct next page (no overlap)
- Cursor handles tied PublishDateOrder via Id tie-breaking
- Updated index test to verify Id column in index

commit.message:
refactor: auto-clean stale registry entries — define errors out of existence

Revert GetStateAsync/GetInfoAsync/GetPersonaAsync from returning null
back to throwing InvalidOperationException for uninitialized grains.
'Not initialized' IS exceptional — it means the registry is stale.

List endpoints now auto-clean the registry when they discover a stale
entry: log a warning and call UnregisterAsync. This makes the error
impossible on the next call — truly defining it out of existence per
Ousterhout's philosophy.

GET /api/groups/{id} and GET /api/agents/{id} catch
InvalidOperationException at the HTTP boundary → 404. This is the
HTTP layer's job: translate domain exceptions to status codes.

No null, no Result<T>, no silent skipping. Stale entries self-heal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: memory.stored breadcrumb lost due to page reload race

Root cause: on conversation.finalized, the UI did location.reload()
after 500ms. Memory extraction takes ~1-2s (LLM call), so the
memory.stored broadcast arrived after the page had already reloaded
and the new WS connection missed it.

Fix: replace location.reload() with ws.close(), which triggers the
existing onclose→reconnect flow. The WS reconnects after 3s, gets
history replay, and stays alive to receive the memory.stored event
that arrives shortly after.

Also reverts the settings.html WS listener added in the previous
commit (that wasn't the actual issue).

refactor: define errors out of existence, remove silent catches

Apply Ousterhout's 'A Philosophy of Software Design' error philosophy:

Define out of existence (no exception, no catch needed):
- GetStateAsync/GetInfoAsync/GetPersonaAsync return null for
  uninitialized grains instead of throwing InvalidOperationException
- Endpoints use null-check instead of try/catch for 404s
- List endpoints filter nulls instead of catching exceptions
- ChatGroupGrain.DeleteAsync and AgentGrain.DeleteAsync drop try/catch
  (orphaned subscriptions are harmless)

Loud errors (log + bubble up):
- LlmIntentGrain: log + rethrow (was silently swallowing LLM failures)
- AgentGrain .Ignore() → .ContinueWith(LogError) for intent grain calls
- Frontend: console.warn on SSE parse failure, console.error on fetch
  failures, console.debug for expected 'group deleted' case

Removed:
- Dead PublishToGroupStream method in OrchestratorService
- All 'catch { /* skip stale entries */ }' patterns
- All silent .catch(() => null) in frontend

11 backend + 26 E2E tests pass.
